commit.message:
Add keyword-level URLs, city-first pattern matching, near-variant dedup, and more negatives (#209)

- Fix stripCityFromActivity to handle city-first keywords (e.g., "oban walking tour"
  now consolidates into "walking tour" group instead of staying as a singleton)
- Add keyword-level final URLs: consolidated ad groups share an RSA but each keyword
  retains its city-specific landing page URL via Google Ads keyword-level finalUrls
- Add near-variant deduplication: normalizes keywords by stripping plurals, stop words,
  and sorting to catch duplicates like "halong bay cruises reviews" vs "halong bay cruise reviews"
- Add negative keywords for brand names (moana sailing, devour barcelona, etc.) and
  informational terms (wildlife nature photography, tank scuba diving, etc.)
- Update Google Ads client to pass finalUrls on AdGroupCriterion
- Update diagnostic report to show keyword-level URLs when they differ from ad-group URL

fix(e2e): repair 13 broken CLI tests + search NameError

CLI tests were failing for two independent reasons:

1. CliRunner fileno crash (tests/e2e/test_cli_commands.py)
   sentence-transformers calls sys.stdout.fileno() internally;
   Typer's CliRunner replaces stdout with StringIO which has no fileno().
   Every test that triggered actual indexing crashed with:
     "Failed to load embedding model: fileno"
   Fix: convert all indexing/search/status tests to use subprocess.Popen
   with `uv --project SRC_ROOT run mvs` (preserves cwd for project
   auto-detection) + communicate(timeout) for safe cleanup.
   Keep CliRunner for init/config/error tests that don't embed.

2. project_name NameError (search.py)
   --project-name option was wired into the CLI decorator and the
   filter-building block inside run_search(), but was never added to
   run_search()'s signature, nor passed at the call site in search_main().
   Every search invocation failed:
     "Search failed: name 'project_name' is not defined"
   Fix: add project_name: str | None = None to run_search() signature
   and pass project_name=project_name at the call site.

Additional test fixes:
- Replace stale chroma.sqlite3 path check with lance/ directory check
- Fix subcommand name: auto-index → index auto
- Fix overly strict "error" assertion: BM25 non-fatal warnings
  contain the word "error"; now checks for "search failed:" specifically
- Increase perf test timeouts (subprocess is slower than CliRunner)

Before: 13 failed, 8 passed, 2 skipped
After:  0 failed, 18 passed, 2 skipped

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Chainlink price feeds as referee layer in /api/prices

Three-source oracle architecture:
  1. CoinGecko (primary) — broad coverage, fast
  2. Chainlink (validator) — decentralized, on-chain, Base mainnet
  3. TruePriceOracle (future) — when our DEX IS the price

- Reads Chainlink AggregatorV3 feeds on Base for ETH, BTC, LINK, USDC, DAI
- Cross-validates: if CoinGecko and Chainlink deviate >1%, flags it
- Auto-override: if deviation exceeds threshold, Chainlink price wins
- 60s Chainlink cache (on-chain reads are heavier)
- ?chainlink=false to skip validation for fast responses
- Response includes validation data (deviationBps, chainlinkAge, etc.)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v3: File-based sync for multi-device support

Records are now JSON files (source of truth), SQLite is a derived index.
Each memory stored as ~/.claude/memory/records/{uuid}.json — syncs via
Syncthing with no conflicts since every file has a unique UUID name.

Key changes:
- UUID primary keys replace integer IDs across all tools and queries
- server.py: startup sync imports new files, removes deleted ones, rebuilds
  connections. Full DB rebuild from files via --rebuild flag
- Automatic migration from v1 (integer IDs) to v2 (UUIDs + JSON files)
- session_end.sh writes JSON record files instead of direct SQLite
- session_start.sh syncs CLAUDE.md from shared config on startup
- Shared config: ~/.claude/memory/config/CLAUDE.md syncs between machines
- settings.yaml + apply_settings.py for shared Claude Code settings
- setup_syncthing.py automates Syncthing folder setup
- .stignore excludes memory.db from sync
- Dashboard updated for UUID-based schema
- 36 tests covering all 7 MCP tools, sync, and rebuild

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ICT Sentinel v5.0 — H4/H1 OB loops, Discount/Premium/OTE, % equity risk

Core improvements:
- H4 + H1 OB scanning via f_scan_obs() loop (not just last bar)
  · Finds real ICT OBs: last candle before impulse move
  · Mitigation check: OBs already traded through are skipped
  · Same function called via request.security for both timeframes
- Discount/Premium filter: longs only below 50% of range, shorts above 50%
- OTE filter: entries only at 61.8-79% Fibonacci retracement (optimal entry)
- Risk changed to % of equity (compounds with account growth, 1% default)
- Judas Swing flag: identifies high-confidence first-session-hour sweeps
- Max 1 trade per day hardened, weekly/daily caps shown in info table
- Fib levels plotted on chart (EQ, OTE long/short zones)
- H4 OB boxes with thick border, H1 OB boxes lighter weight

commit.message:
Add 4KB buffered I/O for file and socket streams

- Socket read/write: 4KB buffers on both POSIX and Amiga (was byte-at-a-time)
- Amiga file read/write: 4KB buffers (each FGetC/FPutC was a DOS library call)
- stream.c: use bulk platform_file_write_buf for FILE write_string
- force-output/finish-output: flush file and socket buffers
- Close flushes write buffers before releasing handles
- Seek invalidates read buffer and flushes write buffer
- file-position accounts for buffered unread/unflushed data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: require Visit Calendar on support contracts, improve visit generation diagnostics

Calendar was not mandatory when creating support contracts, allowing visit
slots to be added to contracts with no calendar assigned. These slots were
then invisible to visit generation (NULL calendar_id never matches).

- Make calendar mandatory for support contracts (client + server validation)
- Block adding visit slots to contracts without a calendar assigned
- Add amber warning banner on contract detail when slots exist but no calendar
- Add detailed diagnostics to visit generation: identifies NULL calendar_id,
  wrong calendar, inactive status, engineer mismatches with actual IDs/names

perf: 3-4x faster embedding job — pipelining, larger batches, higher concurrency

- EMBEDDING_BATCH_SIZE: 10 → 40 (eliminate sequential sub-batching)
- Route batch sizes: 20→40 (images), 30→60 (text-only)
- Time budget: 180s → 240s (use 80% of maxDuration)
- Pipeline compute+write: overlap DB write with next Bedrock compute
- Add timing logs per batch for performance monitoring

ci: add Dockerfile and Cloud Run deploy pipeline

- Multi-stage Dockerfile (eclipse-temurin:21-jdk-alpine build, jre-alpine runtime)
- Non-root user for container security
- GitHub Actions deploy job: builds JAR → Docker image → Artifact Registry → Cloud Run
- Workload Identity Federation (keyless auth — no JSON keys stored in GitHub)
- Secrets injected from GCP Secret Manager at deploy time
- Deploy only on push to main, after build & test passes

usage guidelines.

commit.message:
Fix Railway build: add railway.toml to force Dockerfile builder

Railway defaults to Railpack (auto-detect) and ignores the Dockerfile
unless explicitly told to use Docker. railway.toml sets builder=DOCKERFILE
and configures the health check path so Railway knows when the app is ready.
